Fix Prometheus values indentation bug and Thanos StatefulSet drift

The thanosService insertion misindented storageSpec, scrape configs,
and selector overrides under thanosService instead of prometheusSpec.
This caused Prometheus to lose persistent storage, annotation-based
pod scraping, and label-agnostic ServiceMonitor selection.

Also adds explicit updateStrategy, apiVersion, and kind to Thanos
StatefulSet VCTs to prevent ArgoCD sync drift.

commit.message:
fix: add caching and DB write-back for GBP canonical location ID

- Add module-level in-memory cache (1h TTL) to resolveCanonicalLocationId
  so repeated calls within the same server process skip API calls entirely
- resolveCanonicalLocationId now throws (RATE_LIMITED: prefix or descriptive
  error) instead of silently returning the wrong Place ID on failure
- Track hitRateLimit across all resolution attempts to surface the correct
  error type to callers
- syncGbpReviews and postReply now resolve canonical ID once, write it back
  to social_connections.metadata.locationId (best-effort, fire-and-forget)
  so subsequent syncs skip resolution entirely
- Cron route also resolves and writes back canonical ID per account; rate-
  limit errors are logged as warnings and processing continues to next account
- Remove all three quota/429 fallback blocks in resolveGoogleLocation that
  stored the raw Place ID as locationId — these caused the Reviews API 404
  to repeat on every sync indefinitely

commit.message:
Fix nginx body buffering: keep image uploads in memory, not disk

Nginx logs showed every enroll request triggering:
  "a client request body is buffered to a temporary file"

Default client_body_buffer_size=16KB, but face images are 50KB-2MB.
Every upload was written to disk then read back — 50 concurrent disk
I/O operations crushing throughput.

Fix: client_body_buffer_size=2M keeps images in RAM.
Also: tcp_nopush, tcp_nodelay, worker_rlimit_nofile=8192,
proxy_buffers tuning.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add withRequestConfig to CommonsHttpClient.Builder (#691)

## Summary

Add `withRequestConfig(RequestConfig)` to `CommonsHttpClient.Builder` so
that users can independently configure connect, socket, and
connection-request timeouts on the underlying Apache HttpClient.

## Why

`CommonsHttpClient.Builder.withTimeoutSeconds()` sets all three Apache
HttpClient timeouts (connect, socket, connection-request) to the same
value. Users who need long socket timeouts for slow queries (e.g. 15
minutes) are forced to also set long connect timeouts, which means the
application hangs for that entire duration if a host is unreachable.

Rather than mirroring Apache's `RequestConfig` API one field at a time
on the builder, this PR exposes the `RequestConfig` directly.
`CommonsHttpClient` already exposes other Apache types
(`SSLConnectionSocketFactory`, `PoolingHttpClientConnectionManager`,
`HttpRequestRetryHandler`), so this is consistent with the existing
abstraction boundary. It also means any future `RequestConfig` options
are available without further SDK changes.

## What changed

### Interface changes

- **`CommonsHttpClient.Builder.withRequestConfig(RequestConfig)`** —
accepts a custom Apache `RequestConfig`. When set, it takes precedence
over `withTimeoutSeconds()` and any timeout from `DatabricksConfig`.
- Updated Javadoc on `withTimeoutSeconds()` to reference
`withRequestConfig()` as the fine-grained alternative.

### Behavioral changes

None. Existing users who don't call `withRequestConfig()` get the same
behavior as before.

### Internal changes

- Extracted timeout resolution logic from the constructor into a
`makeDefaultRequestConfig(DatabricksConfig, Integer)` static method for
clarity.

## How is this tested?

- New `testCustomRequestConfig` test in `CommonsHttpClientTest` builds a
client with separate connect (10s), socket (900s), and
connection-request (300s) timeouts and verifies it executes a request
successfully.
- All existing `CommonsHttpClientTest` tests pass.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

tune game rules, rendering, and reward shaping for better training

- Walls rendered as solid filled blocks instead of cross-line style
- Pac-Man can no longer reverse direction (180 turn) unless at a dead
  end, reducing erratic back-and-forth movement
- Apple now spawns at the start of each episode
- Apple spawn interval reduced (420 -> 100 ticks)
- Ghost speed reduced from 75% to 67% of Pac-Man speed
- Death penalty increased (-500 -> -1000)
- Added direction reversal penalty (-5) and exploration bonus (+2 per
  new tile visited)
- Time penalty softened for longer survival
- Updated demo GIF

  for publish, import, and links pages

  table actions column, and add site modal

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix line lookup API returning 'Line not found' for valid lines

The /api/files/:filename/line/:line endpoint was using startOffset to
read a partial file segment, then indexing by global line number into
that segment. This caused line lookups to fail for any line beyond the
segment size. Switch to streaming readline to find the target line
without loading the entire file.

Change-Id: I4507006f1601ee777e5de45d6e31636a3cc60135

fix: deployment gaps — secrets, env vars, K8s config (#37)

* chore(backend): clean up stale vars in .env.example

Keep only AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING and KEY_VAULT_URL, which are
the two vars actually read by the Python code. Remove 11 stale entries
(Schwab creds, hardcoded table names, polling config) that are either
unused, hardcoded, or stored in Key Vault. Add optional tradability
weight vars with their defaults.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(frontend): read VITE_API_BASE_URL env var and fix named import

- client.js: use import.meta.env.VITE_API_BASE_URL with /api fallback
  so the CI/CD-injected env var is honoured at build time
- endpoints.js: fix named import { apiClient } → default import apiClient,
  which was causing a vite build failure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(infra): inject app secrets into K8s pod via secretKeyRef

Add AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING and KEY_VAULT_URL env vars to the
options-backend container, sourced from a K8s Secret named
options-backend-secrets. Without these the pod cannot connect to
Azure Storage or Key Vault at runtime.

To provision the secret in the AKS cluster:
  kubectl create secret generic options-backend-secrets \
    --from-literal=azure-storage-connection-string='<value>' \
    --from-literal=key-vault-url='<value>'

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
